description: One of 2 ogham stones discovered c. 1840 when a flour mill (Mullenroe) was demolished on the site of a possible ringfort in tld of Mountrivers, Co. Cork. The 2nd ogham stone is in the British Museum. This stone was used for a time as a foot bridge over the Delehinagh river. In 1851 it was erected beside nearby St Olan's holy well. description:
This material was made in a open source free software called TextureLab (Link)

4K resolution
PNG format (albedo, normal, roughness, height, AO)
Tileable/seamless
I setup and tested this material in Blender 2.92 in Cycles (experimental) with adaptive subdovision for the optimal display. The textures are embeded in the blend file.

You can also download the textures from [ArtStation](https://www.artstation.com/mrazi/store?tab=products)!

Enjoy!
